description.ko: 갈라진 칼리치 지각과 철빛 실트, 모래, 슬레이트 광물 흔적이 시간의 지형적 기억을 이루는 묵직한 대지 질감 연구입니다.
description.en: A grounded earth-texture study where fractured caliche crust, iron silt, sand, and slate mineral traces form a tactile topographic memory of time.

prompt: TERRA-1, titled "The Caliche Memory Field." A photorealistic close macro study of a weathered caliche hardpan exposed on a dry desert floor, where pale carbonate crust has fractured into shallow raised islands and contour-like rims resembling a relief topographic map made from soil and stone. #8B4513 umber earth appears in undercut seams, #D2691E iron-stained silt wraps around uneven shelves, and powdery #F4A460 sand settles in soft troughs between rough crust plates. Muted #2F4F4F slate mineral speckles collect in tiny pits, pressure lines, and shadowed pores, giving the surface a grounded, time-worn weight. The image emphasizes rough rock texture, compacted soil grains, thin sediment laminations, brittle carbonate flakes, dust caught along ridges, and slow erosion carving memory into the land; low natural side light, no sky, no vegetation, no water, no human objects, hyper-detailed earthy geological texture study.
